researched_category.notes: GoHighLevel (HighLevel) is an all-in-one sales and marketing platform built for agencies, combining CRM, funnels, email/SMS, calendars, automation, AI and white-label SaaS.

one_liner.value: All-in-one CRM, marketing automation, and agency management platform combining contact management, sales pipelines, email/SMS marketing, funnel building, calendar scheduling, and white-label capabilities.

methods.notes: OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code Grant) is the primary/recommended method for public integrations. Private Integration Tokens (PIT) are bearer tokens used for private integrations. Legacy API Keys (v1) are deprecated. The MCP server also supports OAuth and PIT.

access.notes: Free trial has restricted API access (private integration tokens don't work on trial). API access requires a paid plan: Unlimited ($297/mo) for 'Basic API Access' or Agency Pro ($497/mo) for 'Advanced API Access'. Starter ($97/mo) plan does not include API access. No admin/partner/sales approval needed - self-serve signup and payment.

summary.value: Comprehensive REST API v2 with OpenAPI specification covering CRM/Contacts, Conversations, Opportunities, Calendars, Payments, Invoices, Products, Social Planner, Blogs, Emails, Forms, Workflows, Locations, Users and more. Webhook support for 50+ events. Official documentation at marketplace.gohighlevel.com/docs.

agent_interface.notes: Official MCP server from GoHighLevel/LeadConnector at services.leadconnectorhq.com/mcp/ with both OAuth and PIT auth. Also community MCP servers (drausal/gohighlevel-mcp on GitHub). The official MCP supports both a limited core endpoint (/mcp/) and a full per-client v2 endpoint (/mcp/{client}/v2).

rationale.value: GoHighLevel has a well-documented public REST API (v2), an official MCP server, and multiple community MCP servers. The API is broad and covers all major platform features. However, API access requires a paid subscription (Unlimited plan at $297/mo minimum). Free trial accounts have restricted API access. A developer can sign up and pay to get credentials without admin/partner approval, making it 'buildable with access' - the main blocker is the paid plan requirement.

s1.quote: GoHighLevel (often shortened to GHL or HighLevel) is the all-in-one sales and marketing platform built for agencies. Launched in 2018, it consolidates CRM, funnels, email, SMS, calendars, automation, AI and white-label SaaS into a single, unified dashboard
s1.title: What Is GoHighLevel? The Complete 2026 Agency Platform Guide

s2.quote: HighLevel is an all-in-one platform designed to replace multiple subscriptions... CRM, funnel builder, email marketing software, appointment scheduler, SMS marketing tools, and review management software
s2.title: HighLevel Pricing

s3.quote: Complete REST API documentation for the HighLevel CRM platform... OAuth 2.0 and Private Integration setup guides... Webhooks: Real-time notifications for 50+ events
s3.title: HighLevel API Documentation - Developer Portal

s4.quote: OAuth 2.0 is a standard protocol that authorizes a client application... HighLevel supports the Authorization Code Grant flow with v2 APIs.
s4.title: OAuth 2.0 | HighLevel API

s5.quote: Private Integrations allows you to build powerful custom integrations... Private Integration token can be generated easily from the UI... Private Integration tokens are used in the Authorisation header, just like other Access Tokens.
s5.title: Private Integrations: Everything you need to know

s6.quote: Starter $97/Month... Unlimited $297/Month... Basic API Access... Agency Pro $497/Month... Advanced API Access
s6.title: HighLevel Pricing

s7.quote: In addition to the future endpoints that will be released in our OAuth 2.0 API (which is only available in our Advanced API access), this tier unlocks the use of Agency API Keys, where lower plan levels only access Location API Keys.
s7.title: HighLevel API Documentation

s8.quote: Free trial accounts have restricted API access, which is why the private integration key won't confirm. Upgrade to a paid plan to activate full
s8.title: Can free trial use GHL's API key?

s9.quote: Coverage spans your whole LeadConnector account, including: Contacts, Conversations & Messages, Opportunities & Pipelines, Calendars & Appointments, Payments, Products & Store, Invoices & Estimates, Social Planner, Blogs, Emails, Forms & Surveys...and more.
s9.title: LeadConnector MCP Server | HighLevel API

s10.quote: We're excited to announce that the LeadConnector MCP (Model Context Protocol) server is live and ready for use!... The best way to connect your agents to LeadConnector is the per-client endpoint https://services.leadconnectorhq.com/mcp/{client}/v2
s10.title: LeadConnector MCP Server (Official)

s11.quote: The HighLevel MCP Server uses the Model Context Protocol, or MCP, to let AI agents securely interact with HighLevel services... Production MCP Endpoint: https://services.leadconnectorhq.com/mcp/
s11.title: HighLevel MCP Server Setup and Configuration Guide

s12.quote: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server providing access to the complete GoHighLevel API. This server enables AI assistants like Claude to interact with GoHighLevel's services including contacts, opportunities, calendars, workflows, and more.
s12.title: drausal/gohighlevel-mcp (Community MCP Server)

limitations: Could not find explicit documentation that free trial has zero API access - confirmed via community source (Facebook) rather than official docs
The exact difference between 'Basic API Access' and 'Advanced API Access' is not fully documented in official sources beyond tier-based endpoint availability
Composio toolkit presence inferred from broader ecosystem; not directly verified
